          , "reasoning_summary":"> Creating a personalized account settings, using fact-checking tools, and being cautious when sharing information are all crucial steps in managing and dealing with fake content on social media. Firstly, creating a personalized account settings can help to reduce the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. This can be done by adjusting the privacy settings to limit who can see your posts and information, as well as by using the two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security to your account. Secondly, using fact-checking tools can help to identify false information before it spreads further. There are several fact-checking tools available on social media platforms, such as Snopes and FactCheck.org, which can be used to verify the accuracy of a post or claim. Finally, being cautious when sharing information is crucial in preventing the spread of fake content. This means taking the time to research the source of the information before sharing it, as well as being mindful of the language and tone used in the post. By following these steps, individuals can help to manage and deal with fake content on social media in a responsible and effective manner." }

response_parsed.reasoning_summary: > Creating a personalized account settings, using fact-checking tools, and being cautious when sharing information are all crucial steps in managing and dealing with fake content on social media. Firstly, creating a personalized account settings can help to reduce the amount of fake content that appears in your feed. This can be done by adjusting the privacy settings to limit who can see your posts and information, as well as by using the two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security to your account. Secondly, using fact-checking tools can help to identify false information before it spreads further. There are several fact-checking tools available on social media platforms, such as Snopes and FactCheck.org, which can be used to verify the accuracy of a post or claim. Finally, being cautious when sharing information is crucial in preventing the spread of fake content. This means taking the time to research the source of the information before sharing it, as well as being mindful of the language and tone used in the post. By following these steps, individuals can help to manage and deal with fake content on social media in a responsible and effective manner.
